Tests the behavior of Number.prototype.toFixed with NaN as an argument.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion that tests the behavior of the `toFixed` method on the `Number` prototype in JavaScript. It checks if calling `toFixed` with `Number.NaN` as an argument returns the string `"0"`. This test ensures that the `toFixed` method handles `NaN` input correctly by defaulting to zero decimal places. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of the `toFixed` method's behavior.
